name: Crab Gratin
recipeIngredient: 12 oz Crabmeat, picked over to remove cartilage
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Follow package directions to prepare linguine. Cook just until al dente and still slightly firm. Drain at once.
3. Place in a mixing bowl.
4. In a small pot, cook mushrooms with 1 tablespoon of lemon juice and water. Cover the pot with aluminum foil and place on high heat. Cook for about 1 minute until steam begins to escape. Transfer mushrooms to a dish using a skimmer; reserve juices (about 1/4 cup).
5. In a saucepan, stir together flour and clam juice to make a smooth paste. Add remaining clam juice, lemon juice, white wine, and reserved mushroom juices. Season with coriander, dried tarragon, black pepper, and soy sauce.
6. Place over medium heat; bring the liquid to a simmer, stirring constantly. Cook until the mixture thickens (about 3-4 minutes).
7. Stir in heavy cream, parsley, and tomatoes. There will be about 2 1/2 cups total.
8. Lightly oil a 6-cup gratin dish. Distribute mushrooms evenly over the linguine. Pour half of the sauce over the pasta mixture, mixing well.
9. Spread the linguine into a smooth layer. Gently mix remaining sauce with crabmeat and spoon over the linguine. Sprinkle bread crumbs on top.
10. Bake for 15 minutes or until thoroughly heated through. If the top has not browned, briefly broil under high heat. Serve directly from the gratin dish. Serves 4.
